userStories.title: Scaffold browser app
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a minimal browser app scaffold so that the project can render a full-screen experience.
acceptanceCriteria: App starts locally with a single command
A root canvas container is rendered
Production build succeeds
No runtime errors on first load
userStories.notes: Use Vite. Keep the initial scaffold minimal.

userStories.title: Create full-screen canvas shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want the experience to occupy the full viewport so that it feels immersive immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas fills the viewport
Viewport resize updates render size
No scrollbars appear
Canvas is shown on a dark background
userStories.notes: Rendering backend can still be a placeholder.

userStories.title: Read low-entropy browser signals
userStories.description: As a system, I want to read basic browser and device signals so that the scene can be influenced by the current visitor context.
acceptanceCriteria: Read language
Read timezone
Read screen dimensions
Read devicePixelRatio if available
All values stay in memory only
userStories.notes: Do not persist or transmit values.

userStories.title: Read capability and preference signals
userStories.description: As a system, I want to read capability and preference hints so that the scene can adapt to the visitor environment.
acceptanceCriteria: Read hardwareConcurrency if available
Read prefers-color-scheme
Read prefers-reduced-motion
Read touch capability from available browser APIs
All values stay in memory only
userStories.notes: Treat all values as optional.

userStories.title: Expose coarse geo via server endpoint
userStories.description: As a system, I want to expose coarse geolocation hints via a server endpoint so that the client can safely use them without accessing request headers directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Country header is read from Vercel environment
Region header is read from Vercel environment
Geo data is exposed via /api/geo endpoint
Endpoint returns only coarse values (country, region)
Endpoint uses cache-control: no-store
Client does not access request headers directly
App works if geo data is unavailable
userStories.notes: Implement as Vercel serverless/edge function using geolocation().

userStories.title: Track runtime pointer entropy
userStories.description: As a system, I want to collect pointer movement during the session so that the scene can evolve with live visitor interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Pointer position is tracked
Pointer velocity or delta is derived
Mouse and touch both work
No pointer history is persisted
userStories.notes: Normalize coordinates to viewport space.

userStories.title: Generate ephemeral session seed
userStories.description: As a system, I want to combine visitor signals and current time into a session seed so that each visit generates a distinct scene.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed includes current time component
Seed changes on reload
Seed remains stable during one session
Seed is derived without storing source data
userStories.notes: Use a simple deterministic hash.

userStories.title: Define partially legible mapping rules
userStories.description: As a designer, I want explicit mapping rules from visitor signals to visual parameters so that the scene feels personal without revealing raw data.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 4 signal-to-visual mappings are defined
Mappings are indirect rather than literal
Mappings avoid displaying raw identifiers
Mapping rules are documented in code comments or a small config module
userStories.notes: Example: region family to palette temperature, DPR to particle density band, time zone to motion cadence, reduced-motion to calmer motion profile.

userStories.title: Render seeded base scene
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a generated scene based on the session seed so that the work feels unique from the first frame.
acceptanceCriteria: Scene parameters derive from seed
At least one rendered geometry system exists
Scene animates continuously
Scene renders without audio
userStories.notes: Start with one lightweight system such as points or lines.

userStories.title: Map coarse location to palette
userStories.description: As a user, I want geography to subtly influence the visual palette so that the scene feels partially legible.
acceptanceCriteria: Country or region influences palette family when available
Fallback palette is used when geo hints are unavailable
Raw geo values are not displayed in the scene
Palette changes are deterministic within the session
userStories.notes: Use coarse classes instead of exact location labels.

userStories.title: Map device profile to structure
userStories.description: As a user, I want device and browser hints to influence scene structure so that the scene feels linked to the environment I arrived with.
acceptanceCriteria: At least two structural parameters are driven by signals
No raw browser strings are shown
Mapping remains stable within the session
Scene still works if some signals are missing
userStories.notes: Example: touch capability alters curve softness, DPR alters density range.

userStories.title: Add time-based evolution
userStories.description: As a user, I want the scene to change over runtime so that the artwork feels fleeting rather than static.
acceptanceCriteria: Elapsed time changes at least two visual parameters
Scene does not hard reset during runtime
Temporal behavior differs between sessions
Motion remains smooth
userStories.notes: Use seeded modulation curves.

userStories.title: Add pointer influence to scene motion
userStories.description: As a user, I want my pointer movement to alter the scene so that I can perturb the artwork without controlling it directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Pointer changes at least one motion parameter
Effect decays when pointer becomes idle
Touch and mouse both influence the scene
Idle mode still looks intentional
userStories.notes: Treat interaction as entropy, not direct manipulation.

userStories.title: Load audio files from public folder
userStories.description: As a system, I want to load licensed audio tracks from a public folder so that the site can play background music without external services.
acceptanceCriteria: Tracks are loaded from /public/audio
At least one supported audio format is playable
Missing or invalid files fail gracefully
No external streaming service is required
userStories.notes: Prefer mp3 or ogg for browser compatibility.

userStories.title: Randomize track selection
userStories.description: As a user, I want tracks to be selected randomly so that repeated visits do not begin identically.
acceptanceCriteria: A track is chosen randomly at start
Next track is chosen randomly after track end
System handles single-track and multi-track folders
Selection does not require persistent memory
userStories.notes: Avoid immediate repeat when more than one track exists.

userStories.title: Attempt muted autoplay
userStories.description: As a user, I want the audio system to begin in muted mode automatically when allowed so that the visualizer can start without friction.
acceptanceCriteria: Playback is attempted in muted mode
Autoplay failure is handled without crashing
Visuals continue even if autoplay is blocked
Audio can later be enabled by user action
userStories.notes: Respect browser autoplay rules.

userStories.title: Create analyser pipeline
userStories.description: As a system, I want real-time audio analysis so that the scene can react to the music.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio source is connected to an AnalyserNode
Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
Mute state does not disable analysis
userStories.notes: Keep the audio graph simple.

userStories.title: Map bass response to macro motion
userStories.description: As a user, I want low frequencies to influence large-scale movement so that the audiovisual connection feels perceptible.
acceptanceCriteria: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
Response is smoothed to avoid jitter
Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted
No clipping or visual instability occurs
userStories.notes: Examples: camera drift, field expansion, large wave amplitude.

userStories.title: Map treble response to fine detail
userStories.description: As a user, I want high frequencies to influence detail behavior so that the scene reflects the texture of the audio.
acceptanceCriteria: Treble band influences at least one detail property
Response is smoothed to avoid flicker
Effect is visibly distinct from bass mapping
Scene remains readable
userStories.notes: Examples: sparkle, point jitter, line brightness modulation.

userStories.title: Add mute and unmute control
userStories.description: As a user, I want a mute control so that I can choose whether to hear the soundtrack.
acceptanceCriteria: A visible control toggles audio gain
Toggle updates instantly
Control is accessible while the scene is running
Audio analysis continues while muted
userStories.notes: Use a GainNode rather than tearing down playback.

userStories.title: Add info button shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want an info button so that I can discover what the work is about.
acceptanceCriteria: Info button is visible
Button opens an overlay
Overlay closes without reload
Overlay works on desktop and mobile
userStories.notes: Keep UI minimal and unobtrusive.

userStories.title: Write privacy and artwork copy
userStories.description: As a user, I want a short explanation of the artwork and its privacy model so that I understand the intent.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay states this is an art installation
Overlay states no data is stored
Overlay states visitor context influences the scene
Copy stays concise
userStories.notes: Avoid legal overstatement. Keep language simple.

userStories.title: Show partially legible influence hints
userStories.description: As a user, I want subtle examples of what influences the scene so that the mapping feels discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay names categories of influence such as time, device, location, and motion
Overlay does not reveal raw identifier values
Hints align with actual implemented mappings
Hints can be updated from a single source of truth
userStories.notes: Keep this conceptual rather than forensic.

userStories.title: Add reduced-motion fallback
userStories.description: As a user with reduced-motion preference, I want calmer movement so that the work remains comfortable to view.
acceptanceCriteria: Reduced-motion preference is detected
Motion amplitude is lowered
Core visual identity is preserved
No separate page is required
userStories.notes: Do not disable the work entirely.

userStories.title: Add mobile quality scaling
userStories.description: As a user on a mobile device, I want the scene to degrade gracefully so that the site remains usable.
acceptanceCriteria: Lower-capability devices use reduced scene complexity
Touch input remains supported
Mobile browsers do not crash on load
Visual output stays recognizably similar
userStories.notes: Scale particle counts, shader complexity, or post-processing.

userStories.title: Create second visual mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want occasional structural variation so that longer sessions feel less repetitive.
acceptanceCriteria: A second mode is implemented
Both modes reuse the same seed and audio inputs
Mode choice is based on seed or elapsed time
Mode switch does not break playback
userStories.notes: Keep mode boundaries clean so more can be added later.

userStories.title: Add runtime health check
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a lightweight validation pass so that tickets are only closed when the experience runs cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Production build succeeds
No console errors on normal load
Scene renders when audio is blocked
Mute toggle and info overlay both work
userStories.notes: Use this as a recurring definition-of-done gate.

userStories.title: Fix viewport fill on mobile and tablets
userStories.description: As a user on iPhone/iPad, I want the visual canvas to fill the visible viewport so that the artwork feels immersive on mobile devices.
acceptanceCriteria: Visual output fills the viewport on mobile and desktop
Resolution scaling does not shrink the canvas element’s CSS size
Orientation changes update sizing correctly
UI buttons remain reachable and not clipped
userStories.notes: Current implementation multiplies canvas width/height by resolutionScale, which can shrink the canvas on low-tier devices. Resolution scaling must affect render buffer/pixel ratio, not layout size.

userStories.title: Introduce Three.js renderer bootstrap
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a basic Three.js scene (renderer, camera, scene graph) so that 3D visuals can be implemented consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: A WebGL renderer is created and attached to the app container
A perspective camera is configured and renders a placeholder object
A single animation loop renders frames without console errors
Resize updates renderer size and camera aspect correctly
userStories.notes: This story is complete when a minimal 3D scene renders full-screen. No audio-reactivity required yet.

userStories.title: Migrate render loop from Canvas2D to WebGL
userStories.description: As a user, I want the core visual loop to render through WebGL so that 3D depth and point clouds are possible.
acceptanceCriteria: The main render path uses the Three.js renderer each frame
Existing audio analysis values (bass/treble) are still computed
Pointer entropy values are still computed
2D canvas systems are no longer the default visual backend
userStories.notes: Keep existing mappings/seed logic; replace only the draw backend. This reduces risk and keeps privacy rules intact.

userStories.title: Implement 3D point cloud base geometry
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a 3D point cloud with visible depth so that the visual language matches the original intent.
acceptanceCriteria: The scene contains a Points-based 3D point cloud built from BufferGeometry
The point cloud has non-zero Z-depth (not a flat plane)
Depth is perceptible via parallax or camera drift
Point count scales by quality tier (desktop higher, mobile lower)
userStories.notes: Base shape should be generated procedurally (e.g., volume/shell/lattice) and seeded by the session seed. Avoid using external models.

userStories.title: Add audio-driven 3D warp shader for the point cloud
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 3D point cloud to deform and warp in response to music so that the experience feels audio-alive rather than static.
acceptanceCriteria: Bass energy drives macro deformation (e.g., expansion, twisting, or global breathing)
Treble energy drives fine detail (e.g., jitter, sparkle size, micro-displacement)
Warp is time-evolving (not a single repeated shape)
Visuals continue even if autoplay is blocked or audio is muted
userStories.notes: Use GPU-side deformation (ShaderMaterial) so point counts can be higher without CPU bottlenecks.

userStories.title: Increase structural variation within a session
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 3D point cloud to meaningfully change its structure over time so that the experience does not feel like one repeating waveform.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one seeded structural parameter changes over time (not just colour/brightness)
The scene avoids hard resets; transitions feel continuous
Structural evolution remains smooth (no popping)
Different sessions produce noticeably different structures
userStories.notes: Examples: morph between two seeded shapes, slowly change noise frequency, gradually alter camera distance and field radius.

userStories.title: Replace 2D wave mode with a 3D secondary mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want a second 3D mode so that session progression has strong visual variety while staying within the point-cloud aesthetic.
acceptanceCriteria: A second 3D mode is implemented (not a 2D canvas line mode)
Second mode is also audio-reactive (bass + treble mapped distinctly)
Mode selection and switching remain seeded
Switching does not break audio playback or mute control
userStories.notes: Second mode should still be ‘close to point clouds’: e.g., a 3D ribbon of points, a volumetric lattice, or a rotating shell field.

userStories.title: Add smooth visual transitions between 3D modes
userStories.description: As a user, I want mode switches to feel like evolving art rather than a sudden cut so that immersion is preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: Mode switching crossfades or morphs (no sudden hard swap)
Transition duration is short and seeded (e.g., a few seconds)
No frame hitches during transition
Audio continues uninterrupted
userStories.notes: Implement as opacity crossfade or geometry morph. Keep it lightweight; no heavy post-processing is required.

userStories.title: Rework quality scaling to preserve full-screen while reducing GPU cost
userStories.description: As a user on lower-capability devices, I want the work to remain full-screen but cheaper to render so that it remains usable without obvious layout breakage.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas/renderer still fills the viewport on low-tier devices
Quality scaling affects pixel ratio, point count, and shader complexity (not layout size)
Mobile does not crash on load
Visual identity remains recognisable across tiers
userStories.notes: This story replaces the ‘resolutionScale shrinks canvas’ approach with proper render-resolution scaling.

userStories.title: Add viewport safe-area and dynamic viewport handling
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the full-screen experience to work with notches and browser UI changes so that the visuals and controls are not clipped.
acceptanceCriteria: Viewport meta config supports edge-to-edge displays
UI respects safe-area insets (top/bottom) where applicable
Dynamic viewport height changes do not leave permanent blank bars
No scrollbars appear
userStories.notes: Use viewport-fit and safe-area insets; prefer modern dynamic viewport sizing units with fallbacks.

userStories.title: Add minimal visual instrumentation for tuning
userStories.description: As a developer/artist, I want a minimal debug readout so that I can tune point count, warp strength, and audio mappings without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: A debug overlay can be enabled intentionally (e.g., query param)
It shows FPS (or frame time), active mode name, point count, and bass/treble values
It does not display raw visitor identifiers
It is disabled by default
userStories.notes: This is for iteration speed; keep it tiny and removable.

userStories.title: Enforce WebGL-only rendering backend
userStories.description: As a system, I must render the primary visuals using WebGL so that 3D depth and GPU-based deformation are possible.
acceptanceCriteria: Rendering uses Three.js WebGLRenderer
Canvas2D is not used as the primary rendering path
A PerspectiveCamera is present in the scene graph
Rendering fails gracefully only if WebGL is unavailable
userStories.notes: Canvas2D implementations automatically fail this story.

userStories.title: Create volumetric point cloud generator
userStories.description: As a user, I want the scene to contain volumetric 3D geometry so that the visual has real spatial depth rather than a flat surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Base geometry occupies non-zero volume in X, Y, and Z
Geometry is not constrained to a single plane
Point positions are generated in 3D space
Depth is visible through parallax when camera moves
userStories.notes: Allowed base generators: sphere volume, shell, torus volume, 3D noise lattice, spiral field.

userStories.title: Implement shader-based vertex displacement
userStories.description: As a user, I want the geometry to warp fluidly in 3D space so that audio reactivity feels sculptural rather than animated in 2D.
acceptanceCriteria: Geometry deformation occurs in a ShaderMaterial
Bass energy affects large-scale displacement in 3D
Treble energy affects fine-grain displacement or point size
Deformation evolves over time (not static modulation)
userStories.notes: CPU-based per-point loops for deformation are not acceptable for final implementation.

userStories.title: Add autonomous spatial camera motion
userStories.description: As a user, I want the camera to move subtly in 3D space so that depth and structure are perceptible.
acceptanceCriteria: Camera position changes over time
Camera movement reveals parallax across depth
Movement is smooth and seeded
User pointer does not directly control orbit
userStories.notes: OrbitControls may be used for development but not as default interaction.

userStories.title: Implement structural morph targets
userStories.description: As a user, I want the point cloud structure to evolve into different 3D formations during a session so that the experience feels alive.
acceptanceCriteria: At least two distinct volumetric base geometries exist
Scene can interpolate between them smoothly
Morphing does not reset point identity
Morph behavior is seeded and session-unique
userStories.notes: Morphing should occur via shader interpolation or buffer blending.

userStories.title: Replace wave mode with 3D secondary mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want multiple sculptural 3D modes rather than planar wave abstractions.
acceptanceCriteria: Second mode is volumetric and point-based
Mode uses 3D deformation
No 2D line or flat plane geometries remain
Mode switching preserves audio pipeline
userStories.notes: WaveField.ts must be deprecated.

userStories.title: Correct resolution scaling without shrinking layout
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the visual to fill the viewport fully while still reducing GPU cost appropriately.
acceptanceCriteria: Renderer DOM element always matches viewport size in CSS
Quality scaling modifies renderer pixel ratio instead of DOM size
No black bars appear on iPhone or iPad
Orientation changes update renderer and camera correctly
userStories.notes: renderer.setPixelRatio() should be used instead of shrinking canvas width/height.

userStories.title: Introduce atmospheric depth cues
userStories.description: As a user, I want visible depth cues so that the 3D structure feels spatial and immersive.
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation
Point size or brightness varies with distance
Far geometry appears visually distinct from near geometry
Depth effect remains subtle and non-game-like
userStories.notes: Use THREE.Fog or shader depth-based fade.

userStories.title: Adaptive point count scaling
userStories.description: As a user on different devices, I want the point density to adapt without losing the sculptural quality.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop uses significantly higher point count than mobile
Mobile still maintains volumetric depth
Scaling affects geometry complexity, not dimensionality
No fallback to 2D abstraction on low tier
userStories.notes: Low tier may reduce point count but must remain 3D.

userStories.title: Validate shader compilation before scene activation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want custom shaders to compile successfully before they become part of the active render path so that broken WebGL programs cannot ship as completed work.
acceptanceCriteria: All ShaderMaterial vertex and fragment shaders compile without errors
No WebGLProgram VALIDATE_STATUS errors appear during normal startup
No shader compile errors appear in the browser console during normal startup
A ticket involving ShaderMaterial is not complete if any shader compilation error is present
userStories.notes: Definition-of-done gate for all custom shader work.

userStories.title: Enforce shader attribute and uniform integrity
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every shader variable reference to be backed by a declared GLSL symbol and matching runtime binding so that missing attributes or uniforms cannot break rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: Every custom attribute referenced in GLSL is explicitly declared in the shader
Every custom attribute referenced in GLSL is attached to the BufferGeometry at runtime
Every custom uniform referenced in GLSL is declared and initialized before first render
No undeclared identifiers remain in vertex or fragment shaders
userStories.notes: Example failure to prevent: referencing `size` in GLSL without declaring `attribute float aSize;` or a local `float size`.

userStories.title: Add geometry attribute sanity checks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want geometry buffers to be validated before rendering so that malformed point-cloud data cannot produce broken sprites or invisible scenes.
acceptanceCriteria: Position attribute exists and matches expected item size
All required custom attributes exist before material assignment
Geometry initialization rejects NaN or non-finite values in critical buffers
Renderer does not enter the main loop with invalid geometry state
userStories.notes: Critical attributes may include position, size, hue offset, seed, or morph target data.

userStories.title: Create safe fallback point-size path
userStories.description: As a user, I want the point cloud to remain visible even if optional per-point size attributes are absent so that the scene fails gracefully rather than collapsing into broken output.
acceptanceCriteria: Shader supports a valid default point-size path without optional custom size attributes
Scene renders with a uniform base point size when per-point size data is unavailable
Optional size modulation can be enabled only when corresponding attributes are present
Missing optional size attributes do not break shader compilation
userStories.notes: Prefer a guaranteed-safe baseline over clever but brittle shader paths.

userStories.title: Add render startup health gate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want startup rendering health checks so that a scene with invalid shaders or invalid geometry cannot be marked complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Startup verifies shader compilation status before entering steady-state rendering
Startup verifies required geometry attributes before first frame
Startup logs a clear failure reason for shader or geometry errors
Stories touching rendering are not complete if startup health checks fail
userStories.notes: This is a hard stop quality gate, not a debugging convenience.

userStories.title: Add optional debug overlay for shader and geometry state
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a small debug view for render integrity so that broken point-cloud state is diagnosable without reading minified production traces.
acceptanceCriteria: Debug mode can be enabled intentionally
Overlay shows active mode, point count, and whether optional attributes are present
Overlay shows whether shader compile status passed at startup
Overlay is disabled by default and does not expose visitor identifiers
userStories.notes: Keep it tiny and removable.

userStories.title: Add wireframe polyhedra geometry system
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see geometric solids rendered as wireframe edge structures so that the visual vocabulary extends beyond point clouds into architectural, skeletal forms.
acceptanceCriteria: A new GeometrySystem renders 3D polyhedra as LineSegments (not points)
At least 4 base shapes are supported: icosahedron, octahedron, dodecahedron, tetrahedron
Shape selection is seeded per session
Wireframe edges are displaced by the same audio-reactive uniforms as point systems (bass macro, treble micro)
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors
System integrates with modeManager rotation
Depth fog applies to line geometry consistently with point systems
userStories.notes: Use Three.js EdgesGeometry or manual edge arrays for clean wireframe lines. Vertex shader should apply noise-based displacement to edge vertices. This is the foundation for line-based visual modes.

userStories.title: Add constellation connection lines between points
userStories.description: As a user, I want nearby points in the cloud to be connected by lines so that the visual gains structure and web-like constellation effects.
acceptanceCriteria: A connection-line layer can be composited over any point-based geometry system
Lines connect points within a configurable proximity threshold
Connection distance threshold is modulated by bass energy (bass expands/contracts the web)
Line opacity fades with distance between connected points
Connection count is bounded to avoid GPU overload
Lines render as Three.js LineSegments with depth fog
System degrades gracefully on low-tier devices (fewer connections or disabled)
userStories.notes: Inspired by nw_wrld's LowEarthPoint bezier connections. Use a spatial proximity search on the CPU, write results to a line buffer each mode init. Optionally use curved line segments (quadratic bezier tessellated into 3-5 segments) for organic feel.

userStories.title: Add instanced micro-geometry system
userStories.description: As a user, I want the scene to contain many small 3D geometric primitives instead of only points so that the visual texture is richer and more architecturally complex.
acceptanceCriteria: A new GeometrySystem renders many small 3D shapes using Three.js InstancedMesh
At least 3 primitive types are supported: cubes, tetrahedra, octahedra
Primitive type is seeded per session
Per-instance transforms (position, rotation, scale) are audio-reactive
Bass drives macro position displacement across instances
Treble drives per-instance rotation jitter or scale pulse
Instance count adapts to quality tier
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors
userStories.notes: Use InstancedMesh with a custom ShaderMaterial. Instance matrices are updated per frame from a Float32Array. Wireframe overlay on instances (EdgesGeometry) is a bonus but not required for this ticket.

userStories.title: Add fractal recursive geometry generator
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see fractal-like recursive structures so that the visual can produce complex organic or crystalline growths from simple rules.
acceptanceCriteria: A new volumetric shape generator produces recursive subdivision geometry
Recursion depth is seeded (3-6 levels) and adapts to quality tier
At least 2 recursive strategies exist: face subdivision and branching growth
Output is a set of 3D points and/or edge pairs compatible with existing geometry systems
Generated structures are visually distinct from existing volumetric shapes
No stack overflow or excessive allocation for max recursion depth
userStories.notes: Inspired by nw_wrld's CubeGrowth recursive subdivision. Seeded RNG ensures each session produces a unique fractal. Output can feed into pointCloud system (points at vertices) or wireframe system (edge pairs).

userStories.title: Add voronoi cellular fragment shader
userStories.description: As a user, I want a fundamentally different visual texture where points render as faceted cellular regions rather than soft circles so that the shader palette feels broader.
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader renders points/geometry with voronoi cell patterns
Cell boundaries shift with audio energy (bass stretches cells, treble cracks edges)
Color mapping respects the existing palette system (paletteHue, paletteSaturation)
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors
Can be assigned to at least one existing geometry system as an alternative fragment shader
Depth fog still applies
userStories.notes: Voronoi distance field computed in fragment shader using gl_PointCoord or screen-space coordinates. Creates a stained-glass or cracked-ice look vs the current uniform soft-circle aesthetic.

userStories.title: Add chromatic dispersion fragment shader
userStories.description: As a user, I want a prismatic visual effect where RGB channels separate based on audio intensity so that the scene can feel electric and alive during intense audio passages.
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)
Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy
Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy
Color mapping respects the existing palette system
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors
Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems
userStories.notes: Shift R/G/B channels in screen-space or along the point radius using gl_PointCoord offsets. Keep the default state clean; dispersion should only become visible during audio peaks.

userStories.title: Add electric arc line shader
userStories.description: As a user, I want line-based geometry to have animated electrical discharge effects so that wireframe and constellation modes feel energized by audio.
acceptanceCriteria: A vertex shader for LineSegments applies noise-based lateral displacement along line length
Displacement intensity is driven by treble energy (higher treble = more jagged arcs)
Bass energy modulates line brightness or thickness
Arc animation evolves over time (not static noise)
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors
Works with wireframe polyhedra and constellation line systems
userStories.notes: Apply high-frequency simplex noise perpendicular to edge direction in the vertex shader. Use the existing noise3d.glsl library. Creates a Tesla coil / electric discharge aesthetic on wireframe edges.

userStories.title: Add compound mode composition
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see layered visual compositions where multiple geometry systems render simultaneously so that scenes feel richer and more varied across visits.
acceptanceCriteria: ModeManager supports compound modes that layer 2 systems (e.g., pointCloud + constellation, wireframe + particles)
At least 3 compound mode combinations are defined
Compound combinations are seeded per session
Each layer respects its own opacity and quality scaling
Transitions between compound modes use the existing crossfade system
Total GPU cost of compound modes stays within quality tier budget
Mode rotation includes both single and compound modes
userStories.notes: Compound modes are what make nw_wrld feel rich — points + wireframes + connections layered together. Budget-aware: on low tier, compound modes may fall back to single mode.

userStories.title: Add geodesic and platonic solid generators
userStories.description: As a user, I want the wireframe system to include geodesic sphere subdivisions and nested platonic solids so that the geometric vocabulary is architecturally rich.
acceptanceCriteria: Geodesic sphere generator with configurable subdivision level (1-4)
Nested solid generator places scaled copies of the same polyhedron inside each other (2-4 layers)
Dual polyhedra generator creates a shape and its geometric dual overlaid
Output is edge-pair arrays compatible with the wireframe geometry system
Subdivision level adapts to quality tier
Generated edge count stays within performance budget
userStories.notes: Geodesic subdivision of icosahedron is the classic approach. Nested solids (e.g., 3 concentric icosahedra at different scales and rotations) create visually striking depth. Dual overlay (e.g., cube + octahedron) adds geometric complexity.

userStories.title: Add flow-field ribbon shader variant
userStories.description: As a user, I want a shader that creates flowing, tendril-like motion with elongated streaks so that the visual palette includes organic fluid dynamics alongside the current sculptural displacement.
acceptanceCriteria: A new vertex shader displaces points along curl noise flow-field lines
Points are rendered as elongated sprites aligned to their velocity direction
Flow speed is modulated by bass energy
Flow turbulence is modulated by treble energy
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors
Can be assigned to particleField or pointCloud systems
Visual character is distinctly different from existing warp shaders
userStories.notes: Use curl3 from noise3d.glsl for divergence-free flow. Elongated sprites can be achieved via gl_PointSize modulation along the velocity axis or by rendering short line segments instead of points. Creates a smoke/aurora/tendril aesthetic.

userStories.title: Remove instanced micro-geometry system
userStories.description: As a user, I do not want to see tiny 3D primitive shapes used as particles because they look low-resolution and visually cheap compared to point-based and line-based rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: The microGeometry system (US-056) is removed from the mode rotation
Its shader files, system file, and generator code are deleted
ModeManager no longer references microGeometry as a mode
No instanced mesh rendering of small cubes/tetrahedra/octahedra remains
Build passes and no dead imports remain
userStories.notes: US-056 was a wrong direction. Geometric complexity should come from connections between points, not from replacing points with tiny meshes. This ticket cleans up the failed approach.

userStories.title: Rework wireframe system to vertex-and-edge topology
userStories.description: As a user, I want geometric shapes to be formed by visible dots at vertices connected by lines along edges so that the shapes emerge from the connection structure rather than appearing as standalone wireframe meshes.
acceptanceCriteria: Wireframe polyhedra render as Points at vertex positions AND LineSegments connecting them along edges
Both the vertex dots and the connecting edge lines are visible simultaneously
Vertex dots use the existing point rendering pipeline (soft circular points with fog)
Edge lines use LineSegments with matching color and fog
The geometric shape is clearly recognizable through its vertex-edge structure
Audio reactivity displaces vertices, and connected edges follow (they share positions)
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors
userStories.notes: The key insight from nw_wrld: geometric forms are made of dots + connecting lines, not filled wireframe meshes floating in space. Render a Points mesh for vertices and a LineSegments mesh for edges, sharing the same transformed positions. The shape identity comes from the topology of connections.

userStories.title: Add recursive fractal growth wireframe structures
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see complex fractal wireframe structures that grow recursively from a seed shape so that the visual includes organic architectural forms made of connected vertices.
acceptanceCriteria: A generator produces recursive cube-growth structures inspired by nw_wrld's CubeGrowth
Starting from a seed cube, faces spawn child cubes with seeded recursion depth (3-5 levels)
Each cube is defined as 8 vertex positions and 12 edge index pairs
All cube instances are merged into a single vertex buffer and a single edge-line buffer
Vertices render as Points, edges render as LineSegments (per US-065 pattern)
Recursion branching is controlled by noise so each session produces unique growth patterns
Total vertex/edge count adapts to quality tier
Structure is audio-reactive (vertices displaced by bass/treble)
userStories.notes: Direct adaptation of nw_wrld's CubeGrowth technique: explicit CUBE_VERTICES (8 corners) and CUBE_EDGES (12 index pairs), each instance transformed by a 4x4 matrix from the recursive growth algorithm. All instances merged into one LineSegments draw call. Optional depth-occlusion via invisible instanced occluder meshes (colorWrite:false, depthWrite:true).

userStories.title: Add Bezier curve web connections between scattered points
userStories.description: As a user, I want scattered points to be connected by curved arcing lines so that the visual creates a luminous web of organic connections rather than straight-line proximity links.
acceptanceCriteria: A connection system draws quadratic Bezier curves between pairs of points
Curves arc outward from the midpoint (control point offset perpendicular to the pair axis)
Each curve is tessellated into 4-6 straight line segments for smooth rendering
Connection pairs are selected from a subset of points (not all-to-all) to control density
Line opacity is low (0.05-0.15) so individual curves are subtle but overlapping density creates a visible web
Arc curvature is modulated by bass energy (stronger bass = more pronounced arcs)
Connection count adapts to quality tier
Works as a composable layer over any point-based geometry system
userStories.notes: Direct adaptation of nw_wrld's LowEarthPoint technique. The key: curves between point PAIRS using quadratic Bezier with a midpoint control pushed outward (midZScale). At low individual opacity but high pair count, the overlapping curves create a glowing web/mesh effect. This replaces the straight-line proximity approach of US-055 with something more organic.

userStories.title: Add spatial cube lattice wireframe system
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a 3D grid of wireframe cubes forming a spatial lattice so that the visual includes structured architectural grid forms made of connected vertices.
acceptanceCriteria: A generator produces an NxNxN grid of cube wireframes (e.g. 4x4x4 or 6x6x6)
Each cube is defined by 8 vertices and 12 edges, merged into combined buffers
Grid dimensions are seeded per session
Vertices render as Points, edges render as LineSegments
Audio-reactive vertex displacement warps the grid (bass = macro warp, treble = vertex jitter)
Grid size adapts to quality tier
Optional: quadrant subdivision outlines at larger scale overlay the fine grid
userStories.notes: Inspired by nw_wrld's CubeCube: a regular 3D grid of cube wireframes. The uniform structure contrasts with the organic fractal growth of US-066. When audio-displaced, a regular grid warping into organic shapes is visually striking. Shared vertices between adjacent cubes should be merged to avoid duplicate points.

userStories.title: Rework constellation lines to form recognizable geometric topologies
userStories.description: As a user, I want the constellation connection system to form recognizable geometric shapes through its connections rather than random proximity webs so that the visual has structural intention.
acceptanceCriteria: Constellation system places points at vertices of known geometric topologies (tetrahedra, octahedra, icosahedra clusters)
Edges connect vertices according to the topology's edge table, not proximity threshold
Multiple small topology instances are scattered in 3D space with seeded positions and orientations
Each topology instance is clearly recognizable as a geometric form
Topology type and count are seeded per session
Audio reactivity displaces the topology vertices (edges follow)
Instance count adapts to quality tier
userStories.notes: The current constellation system (US-055) uses distance-threshold proximity which creates random webs with no recognizable structure. This rework places points deliberately at topology vertices and connects them via explicit edge tables. Think: a field of small connected icosahedra and tetrahedra floating in space, each one clearly a geometric shape made of dots and lines.

userStories.title: Add depth-occluded wireframe rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want wireframe structures to have proper hidden-line removal so that edges behind solid faces of the shape are occluded, giving the geometry more readable spatial depth.
acceptanceCriteria: An optional occluder pass renders invisible solid geometry (colorWrite:false, depthWrite:true) for wireframe shapes
Edge lines use depthTest:true, depthWrite:false so they respect the occluder depth buffer
Occluded edges are hidden, making the 3D structure of wireframe shapes much clearer
Occlusion can be toggled per geometry system
Performance cost is bounded (instanced occluder mesh for repeated shapes)
Works with recursive growth, lattice, and topology-based wireframe systems
userStories.notes: Direct adaptation of nw_wrld's CubeGrowth occlusion technique. An InstancedMesh of solid shapes with colorWrite:false writes to the depth buffer without drawing any color. Edge LineSegments then respect this depth buffer, hiding edges that would be behind solid faces. This transforms see-through wireframes into readable 3D objects.

userStories.title: Fix cluttered multi-polyhedra constellation scene
userStories.description: As a user, I do not want to see many tiny line-filled ball shapes scattered together because the result reads as visual noise rather than recognizable geometry.
acceptanceCriteria: Constellation topology system (US-069) is reworked: instance count drops dramatically (1-3 large hero topologies, not a field of small ones)
Each remaining topology instance is significantly larger and centered with clear silhouette
Instances do not overlap in screen space (seeded positions enforce minimum separation)
Edge count per instance is reduced if a topology produces dense crossing lines (prefer icosahedron/octahedron over dual/compound forms here)
No console errors; quality tiers still respected
Visual is recognizably a small number of clean geometric solids, not a tangled web
userStories.notes: User feedback: current implementation looks 'really crappy and convoluted' — multiple lines inside multiple ball shapes. Fewer, larger, clearly separated instances will read as intentional geometry.

userStories.title: Fix boring cube lattice wireframe appearance
userStories.description: As a user, I do not want the cube lattice mode to look like a uniform grid of cubes connected by wobbly edges because it reads as repetitive and uninteresting.
acceptanceCriteria: Cube lattice (US-068) is reworked so the visible structure is no longer a uniform NxNxN cube grid
Either: lattice cells are non-uniformly scaled/skewed, OR a noise-driven mask hides a meaningful fraction of cells to create voids and silhouettes
Edge displacement is bounded so lines remain straight-ish at rest and only deform meaningfully on bass hits (no constant 'wobble')
Vertex points along the lattice are visibly emphasized at intersections
Mode still passes shader integrity gate and quality scaling
Result reads as an architectural structure, not a grid of identical cubes
userStories.notes: User feedback: 'just boring, simply a multitude of cubes connected to each other with wobbly edges'. Break uniformity and reduce idle wobble.

userStories.title: Add wireframe terrain heightfield generator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a generator that produces a planar grid mesh deformed into hills and valleys so that a terrain-like wireframe geometry can be rendered.
acceptanceCriteria: New generator under src/visual/generators/ produces an MxN grid of vertices on a plane with seeded extents
Y (height) of each vertex is set by layered noise (e.g. fbm of curl/simplex) producing visible hills and valleys
Generator outputs both vertex positions and edge index pairs (grid edges only) for LineSegments
Grid resolution adapts to quality tier
Output is deterministic per session seed
Unit-tested with a basic shape/extent assertion or smoke test
userStories.notes: Reference image in docs/59c1980a-68db-41c8-9549-f96245e88049.png shows the target: a wireframe terrain plane with rolling hills. Pure data generator only; rendering system is US-074.

userStories.title: Add terrain heightfield render system with audio reactivity
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a wireframe terrain plane with rolling hills and valleys so that the scene includes a recognizable landscape geometry.
acceptanceCriteria: New system under src/visual/systems/ renders the US-073 heightfield as Points (vertices) + LineSegments (grid edges)
Camera framing shows the terrain in perspective with visible depth toward a horizon
Bass energy drives macro vertical displacement (waves rolling across the terrain)
Treble drives fine vertex jitter / point size shimmer
Mode is registered with ModeManager and selectable in rotation
Shader integrity gate passes; no WebGL errors; runs in baseline mode without modulation
Quality tiers reduce grid density rather than canvas size
userStories.notes: Matches the docs/ reference image. This is the headline new visual.

userStories.title: Add spatial gradient palette for position-based vertex coloring
userStories.description: As a user, I want different regions of a geometry to be colored differently with smooth transitions so that the scene has rich gradients of bright color rather than a single hue.
acceptanceCriteria: Palette module exposes a function that returns a vertex color from a normalized world-space position (e.g. by X or radial distance)
Gradient interpolates through 3-5 bright stops chosen from the seeded palette family
A vertex color attribute can be attached to BufferGeometry by any system that opts in
Point and line shaders read the color attribute when present and fall back to the existing uniform color path when absent
The terrain system (US-074) opts in and shows visible color zones across the surface
No regression in modes that do not opt in
userStories.notes: Reference image shows blue → purple → orange gradient across the terrain. Implement as an opt-in vertex-color path so other systems (e.g. cube lattice, fractal growth) can adopt later without breakage.
